_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how repetition, rhyme and rhythm make simple poems, chants and songs flow smoothly and hang together.

_source.w: We are learning how repetition, rhyme and rhythm make simple poems, chants and songs flow smoothly and hang together.
y: Repetition and rhyme make language fun and memorable.
Understanding these patterns helps us enjoy and create our own poems and songs.
These language features help hold a text together so it feels complete.
eg: We listen to a poem and clap every time we hear a repeated word or line.
We find the rhyming words in a song and list them on the board.
We notice how a chant repeats a line to create a pattern.
We create our own short poem using repeated lines.
We tap out the rhythm of a favourite song and feel how the beat holds it together.
We compare two poems — one with rhyme and one without — and talk about how they feel different.
